value.primary_driver: profit-taking after AI-related rallies and renewed technology valuation concerns
value.regional_pattern: Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong, and mainland China fell; Australia rose on a cash-close basis

market_session: Asia regular cash-equity sessions, Friday 2026-06-26, all closed
text: Friday, June 26, 2026 was treated as a regular trading day for all referenced Asian cash-equity venues. AP reported that shares plunged in Asia, led by heavy losses in Japan and South Korea, as traders sold to lock in gains from recent AI-related rallies. Reuters reported MSCI Asia ex-Japan fell nearly 3%. Australia was the exception, closing higher on the ASX 200 cash-market basis.

nikkei_225.basis: Trading Economics news-stream close basis. TE CFD table separately showed 69,175 / -4.41%; Yahoo Finance historical cash data separately showed 69,360.88 / -4.15%; AP corroborated direction and magnitude at -4.4%.

hang_seng.basis: Trading Economics HK50 table Jun/26: 22,671.86 / -405.05 / -1.76%; Yahoo Finance history corroborated the close.

shanghai_composite.basis: Xinhua official close summary: Shanghai Composite down 2.26% to 4,027.26.

asx_200.basis: Yahoo Finance cash-market basis: quote page previous close 8,748.70; historical close 8,764.20; calculated point change +15.50 and rounded percent +0.18%. Trading Economics ASX CFD/news basis is disclosed separately and not mixed into this calculation.

market_session: Asia regular cash-equity closes, Friday 2026-06-26; Nikkei noted on Trading Economics news-stream basis; ASX on Yahoo cash-close basis

calculation.method: ASX cash calculation: 8,764.20 close minus 8,748.70 previous close equals +15.50; 15.50 / 8,748.70 = +0.177%, rounded to +0.18%. Other listed index changes use directly reported source tables/news summaries.

text: Asian equity indexes closed mostly lower on Friday, June 26, 2026. Trading Economics reported the Nikkei 225 news-stream close at 69,095, down 3,271 points / -4.52%, KOSPI at 8,411.21, down 519.09 / -5.81%, and Hang Seng at 22,671.86, down 405.05 / -1.76%. Xinhua reported the Shanghai Composite down 2.26% to 4,027.26. The ASX 200 was the regional exception on a Yahoo cash-close basis, closing at 8,764.20, up 15.50 points / +0.18% from a prior close of 8,748.70.

japan.basis: Trading Economics Japan news stream and component table Jun/26.

south_korea.basis: Trading Economics South Korea news stream Jun/26.

hong_kong.basis: Trading Economics Hong Kong news stream Jun/26.

value.driver: Profit-taking after AI-related rallies, renewed Wall Street technology weakness, and concern that Apple price hikes signaled structural inflation from AI spending and limited tech components.

text: The Friday Asia selloff was concentrated in AI, semiconductor, and hardware-linked stocks. Trading Economics reported SoftBank Group -12.19%, Taiyo Yuden -11.48%, Advantest -10.67%, and Tokyo Electron -4.26% in Japan; Samsung Electronics -6.28%, SK Hynix -8.95%, and SK Square -9.43% in South Korea; and SMIC -6.8% in Hong Kong. AP framed the move as profit-taking after AI rallies, while Reuters tied global pressure to Apple price hikes and concerns about AI-driven structural inflation and component scarcity.

value.ipo_context: Lingyi iTech debuted in Hong Kong with an HK$8.3 billion IPO, but the listing did not improve broader sentiment.

text: Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 405.05 points / -1.76% to 22,671.86. Trading Economics said technology, financial, and producer manufacturing shares came under pressure as AI and semiconductor-related selling weighed on regional sentiment. Tencent fell 2.28%, AIA 1.94%, Xiaomi 3.95%, Meituan 2.80%, SMIC 6.8%, and Kingboard Laminates 5.2%. Lingyi iTech's HK$8.3 billion Hong Kong IPO did not improve broader sentiment.

value.sector_pattern: Education and pork sectors led gains; cobalt metal and battery industry posted sharp declines.

text: Mainland Chinese benchmarks closed lower on Friday. Xinhua reported the Shanghai Composite down 2.26% to 4,027.26, the Shenzhen Component down 3.44% to 15,782.22, the ChiNext down 4.07% to 4,194.21, and the STAR Composite down 2.02% to 2,343.66. Combined turnover reached 3.55 trillion yuan, about $521 billion, down from 3.59 trillion yuan in the prior session. Education and pork shares led gains, while cobalt metal and battery-industry shares posted sharp declines.

asx_200_cash_basis.basis: Yahoo Finance quote and historical pages.

asx_200_trading_economics_context.basis: Trading Economics news stream and CFD stats table; disclosed separately from Yahoo cash calculation.

market_session: ASX regular cash-equity close, Friday 2026-06-26; cash basis from Yahoo Finance

calculation.method: 8,764.20 Yahoo historical close minus 8,748.70 Yahoo previous close equals +15.50; +15.50 / 8,748.70 = +0.177%, rounded to +0.18%. Trading Economics CFD actual 8,765.80 and prior 8,748.65 are tracked separately as CFD context.

text: Australia was the regional exception. On a Yahoo cash-market basis, the ASX 200 closed at 8,764.20, up 15.50 points / +0.18% from a previous close of 8,748.70. Trading Economics separately reported that the ASX 200 edged up 16 points / 0.2% to close at 8,764, and its CFD table showed 8,765.80 / +0.20% from 8,748.65. The positive Australian move was supported by miners, with BHP +0.8%, Rio Tinto +2.2%, and Northern Star and Evolution Mining each up about 3%, while CSL fell 2.36%. The ASX still fell 0.7% for the week.

tokyo_core_cpi.basis: XTB and Trading Economics context; first acceleration in eight months.

market_session: Asia session Friday 2026-06-26 with Reuters NY wrap cross-asset reference
text: Reuters reported the yen near its weakest level in 40 years at 161.76 per dollar, while the dollar index eased 0.16% to 101.35 and the euro rose 0.14% to $1.1385. US Treasury yields fell, with the 10-year at 4.38% and the 2-year at 4.096%. Tokyo core CPI accelerated to 1.6% year over year from 1.3%, and XTB reported core-core CPI rising to 1.9% from 1.6%, reinforcing expectations that the Bank of Japan could continue raising interest rates.

brent_crude.basis: Reuters: Brent crude futures fell 4.34% to settle at about $72 per barrel.

hormuz_shipping.basis: Reuters reported more tankers left the Strait of Hormuz and Saudi Aramco resumed oil loading at Ras Tanura after a nearly four-month halt, while a cargo vessel was hit near Oman.

value.australia_link: Trading Economics said rising copper and gold prices supported miners, with BHP +0.8%, Rio Tinto +2.2%, and Northern Star and Evolution Mining each about +3%.

market_session: Reuters NY close cross-asset reference for Friday 2026-06-26
text: The cross-asset backdrop was mixed. Reuters reported Brent crude futures down 4.34% to settle at about $72 per barrel as more tankers left the Strait of Hormuz and Saudi Aramco resumed oil loading at Ras Tanura, even though a cargo vessel was hit near Oman. Spot gold rose 1.06% to $4,068.72 per ounce. Lower oil was not enough to prevent heavy selling in Japan and Korea, but stronger gold and copper helped explain Australia's relative outperformance through miners such as BHP, Rio Tinto, Northern Star, and Evolution Mining.
